title: molten polish kombu roast
description: A nutty, molten, cozy Polish dish featuring kombu and roast, halal-friendly, perfect for a weeknight dinner.
instructions: Rinse the kombu sheets and soak them in water for 30 minutes.
Pat dry the kombu sheets and cut them into smaller pieces.
Season the beef roast with salt and pepper.
Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the beef roast until browned on all sides, then set it aside.
Add the sliced onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the caraway seeds and flour to the pot, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the kombu pieces and beef broth to the pot, and stir to combine.
Return the beef roast to the pot, cover it with a lid, and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 2 hours, or until the beef is tender and falls apart easily.
Remove the pot from the oven and carefully remove the beef roast from the pot.
Serve the beef roast with the kombu sauce spooned over the top.
